system.name: Blade Runner RPG
system.description: Criminal investigators in 2037 Los Angeles pursuing Replicants and uncovering conspiracies in a neon-drenched future noir world. You roll two polyhedral dice (attribute + skill), looking for 6+ as successes.

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.description: Muscle power, toughness, and physical endurance.

attributes.name: Agility
attributes.description: Body control, speed, and fine motor skills.

attributes.name: Empathy
attributes.description: Social intelligence, charisma, and emotional stability.

skills.name: Force
skills.description: Lifting, pushing, breaking obstacles. Any feat of brute strength.

skills.name: Hand-to-Hand Combat
skills.description: Melee attacks and defense using body or melee weapons. Opposed rolls in close combat.

skills.name: Stamina
skills.description: Physical endurance, resisting poison, surviving underwater, death saves.

skills.name: Firearms
skills.description: All ranged weapon attacks. Straight skill roll (not opposed).

skills.name: Mobility
skills.description: Climbing, jumping, sprinting, chases, risky movement.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.description: Sneaking, hiding, pickpocketing. Opposed vs. Observation when detected.

skills.name: Medical Aid
skills.description: First aid on Broken characters, stabilizing critical injuries, healing.

skills.name: Observation
skills.description: Spotting clues, detecting ambushes, examining crime scenes.

skills.name: Tech
skills.description: Hacking, repairs, decryption, programming, Esper photo enhancement.

skills.name: Connections
skills.description: Street contacts, finding people, requisitioning LAPD gear, black market deals.

skills.name: Manipulation
skills.description: Persuasion, intimidation, interrogation. Opposed vs. Insight when resisted.

skills.name: Insight
skills.description: Reading emotions, detecting lies, resisting manipulation, Baseline Tests (Replicants).

skills.name: Driving
skills.description: Vehicle maneuvers. Uses vehicle's Maneuverability stat instead of a character attribute.

dice.mechanic: Roll 2 Base Dice (1 attribute die + 1 skill die). Results of 6+ are successes. 10+ on a single die counts as 2 successes.

pushing.description: Re-roll any dice not showing a 1. Each 1 on pushed roll inflicts damage (STR/AGL) or stress (INT/EMP).
pushing.replicantNote: Replicants always suffer stress (never damage) from pushing, even on STR/AGL rolls.

dice.advantage: Add a third Base Die (same type as your lower die). Roll 3 dice total.
dice.disadvantage: Remove your lower Base Die. Roll only 1 die.

options.name: Human
options.notes: Can be any archetype except Doxie.

options.name: Replicant
options.bonusAttributeRestriction: Must be spent on Strength or Agility.
options.yearsOnForce: Always Rookie (only 1 year since N-9 models approved on Earth).
options.notes: Can be any archetype except Cityspeaker and Skimmer. May not know they are a Replicant (GM rolls secretly on D6: 1 = unaware).

archetypes.name: Analyst
archetypes.description: Forensics specialist. Master of the crime lab. Chemistry, pathology, ballistics, criminology. You wring truth from evidence.
suggestedAppearances: A pristinely kept tragedy taped off from the public.
OCD and a god complex wrapped in an emotionally guarded scarf.
Your mom says you're a catch.

archetypes.name: Cityspeaker
archetypes.description: Deep undercover operative. You know every syndicate, scumbag, and slumlord on a first-name basis. The city speaks to you.
suggestedAppearances: You look like street trash, because you are street trash.
The ugliest side of humanity on the outside. Its greatest hope on the inside.
A Dickensian acid trip.

archetypes.name: Doxie
archetypes.description: Hyper-aware infiltrator built for seduction and assassination. Body language, vocal tones, pulse rate. You detect and project anything the case demands.
suggestedAppearances: You are a thing of beauty. Quite literally.
All they see are those sad, soulful eyes.
You don't mind that people don't look you in the eyes. They'd otherwise notice how closely you're studying them.

archetypes.name: Enforcer
archetypes.description: Tactical operator. CDP veteran. When real bad-asses take the stage, it's your job to give 'em a show. Beer or battering ram, your choice.
suggestedAppearances: Ass-kicking boots. The rest is whatever wasn't dirty or bloody from the day before.
You're human C4. A drab, mushy square that's sexiest when it's armed.
You only have two outfits: gym clothes and riot gear.

archetypes.name: Fixer
archetypes.description: Political operator. Master of manipulation. You tear through red tape, leap through loopholes, and quell every controversy. The only case you're working is your own.
suggestedAppearances: Everything you wear is a statement. And they all say either Screw You or You Wish.
You dress for whatever you want today. And you've got a walk-in closet.
You walk, talk, and dress like somebody who is going to fire everybody you know one day.

archetypes.name: Inspector
archetypes.description: Classic noir detective. Trench coat. Hip flask. Pushing buttons and picking wrong fights. A walking Voight-Kampff machine.
suggestedAppearances: That old trenchcoat reflects you. There's a desire or regret behind every scuff, stain, and tear.
A smirk that cuts glass. A stare that stretches for miles.
You can't stop fidgeting with the dent in your finger where your wedding ring used to be.

archetypes.name: Skimmer
archetypes.description: Dirty cop. You owe half the city and the other half owes you. Side hustles, planted evidence, special interests. You're not clean, but you get the job done.
suggestedAppearances: I got mustard on my face or somethin'?
People can tell what you're getting up into by what you smell like.
You look like you live off shitty coffee, shitty takeout, and shitty people. And you're in your prime.

yearsOnForce.name: Rookie
yearsOnForce.description: Fresh on the force. High physical potential, fewer tricks up your sleeve.

yearsOnForce.name: Seasoned
yearsOnForce.description: A few years in. You've seen things.

yearsOnForce.name: Veteran
yearsOnForce.description: Hardened by years on the streets.

yearsOnForce.name: Old-Timer
yearsOnForce.description: You've seen it all. Fewer physical gifts, but deep skill and experience.

health.example: STR B (10) + AGL C (8) = 18/4 = 4.5, rounded up = 5. Replicant adds +2 = 7.

resolve.example: INT B (10) + EMP D (6) = 16/4 = 4. Replicant subtracts 2 = 2.

specialties.name: Bodyguard
specialties.description: If someone within SHORT range is hit, roll MOBILITY to dive in and take the hit for them.

specialties.name: Cashflow
specialties.description: Off-the-books income. Gain a Chinyen Point at the start of every Case File.

specialties.name: Controlled
specialties.description: When rolling for a critical stress effect, you may re-roll the result once.

specialties.name: Counselor
specialties.description: Once per Shift, heal 1 stress from another character by talking to them. Cannot self-target.

specialties.name: Fast Reflexes
specialties.description: Draw an additional initiative card at the start of combat. Choose which to use.

specialties.name: Gutter Rat
specialties.description: Move to a new location in LA on foot without rolling CONNECTIONS.

specialties.name: Hardened
specialties.description: Maximum Resolve +1. Can take this specialty up to three times.

specialties.name: Hip Flask
specialties.description: Once per Shift, take a swig to heal 1 stress.

specialties.name: Insider
specialties.description: Advantage on all CONNECTIONS rolls to acquire gear or resources from the LAPD.

specialties.name: Interrogator
specialties.description: Advantage on all MANIPULATION rolls to extract info from a prisoner or court witness.

specialties.name: Kickbacks
specialties.description: Advantage on all CONNECTIONS rolls to buy or sell on the black market.

specialties.name: Killer
specialties.description: Roll an extra Crit Die when inflicting a critical hit, choose preferred result.

specialties.name: Married to the Job
specialties.description: Go 4 Shifts without Downtime before suffering stress (instead of normal 3).

specialties.name: Martial Arts
specialties.description: Advantage on all HAND-TO-HAND COMBAT rolls when fighting unarmed.

specialties.name: Merciful
specialties.description: When inflicting a critical injury, you can voluntarily reduce the Crit Die to make it less lethal.

specialties.name: Musician
specialties.description: Advantage on MANIPULATION rolls where singing or playing an instrument is helpful.

specialties.name: Origami
specialties.description: Fold an origami figure (few minutes) to heal 1 stress. Once per Shift.

specialties.name: People Person
specialties.description: Gain a second key relationship with the same rules effects as your first.

specialties.name: Protected
specialties.description: When you lose a Promotion Point, roll CONNECTIONS. Each success reduces points lost by 1 (min 0).

specialties.name: Scientist
specialties.description: Advantage on TECH or MEDICAL AID rolls when making scientific assessments.

specialties.name: Smokes
specialties.description: Once per Shift, light up to heal 1 stress.

specialties.name: Sniper
specialties.description: Advantage on FIREARMS rolls when firing a single shot at LONG range or more, from a hidden position.

specialties.name: Sycophant
specialties.description: Gain an additional Promotion Point at the end of each session by ingratiating yourself with superiors.

specialties.name: Tough
specialties.description: Maximum Health +1. Can take this specialty up to three times.

keyMemory.description: A defining memory from the past. May be real, someone else's, or a designed implant (Replicants). Once per session, use it for advantage on a skill roll. Interacting with it earns a Humanity Point.

options.text: When you were a small child. The memory is now a fleeting vision.
During childhood. You remember it like it was yesterday.
When you were a young teen. The event defined your view of the adult world.
When you were an older teenager, turning into a young adult.
Standing in the rain on a city street corner in downtown LA.
In a lavish penthouse suite overlooking the Fashion District.
While ogling the synthetic fish tanks on Animoid Row.
Lost in a seething crowd in a loud, dimly lit nightclub.
In a serene, exclusive corporate headquarters.
In the derelict housing projects of Los Angeles Hills.
Off the grid in the Kipple wastelands.
On a protein farm deep within the Energy Empire.
On a distant farmstead, in the stillness of the rural countryside.
In a wintry landscape, covered by snow.
In a foreign land, surrounded by crowds speaking a language you didn't understand.
You saw something extraordinary that you cannot explain.
You took part in a violent crime, or witnessed one.
You took part in a battle or a massacre.
You were physically attacked or abused.
You suffered a breach of trust or betrayal.
You suffered or witnessed a horrible accident.
You made or witnessed a selfless sacrifice.
You suffered a moment of weakness.

keyRelationship.description: An NPC who plays a big role in your life. Can be living, dead, artificial, or fictitious (memory implant). Used by GM to create events. Interacting with them earns Humanity Points.

options.text: You're in a fight about something.
They want you to quit your job.
You think they are hiding something.
They are suspected of a crime.

signatureItem.description: A small sentimental item connected to your key memory or relationship. Interact with it to recover 1 stress per game session.

options.text: An open stool at your favorite bar

standardGear.name: Knowledge Integration Assistant (KIA)
standardGear.description: Handheld computer. Share clues, access LAPD Mainframe, communicate with partner.

standardGear.name: Detective Special Spinner
standardGear.description: Your flying car. Reaches anywhere in LA in minutes.

options.text: A sparsely furnished LAPD housing apartment in Sector 5, small and claustrophobic.
A condo filled with old mementos and a balcony overlooking the neon-lit streets of Little Tokyo.
A cheap run-down hotel room by Hawker's Circle, with flickering lights and dripping water pipes.
A huge but dilapidated old apartment in an abandoned building on Retirement Row, with leaking ceilings and full of debris.
In a dingy flat on top of a seedy bar off Nightclub Row.
In a cluttered warehouse off Animoid Row, hidden from prying eyes.
In a serene luxury penthouse hotel suite in the Financial District.
You don't have a home, drifting around and spending every night in a different place.
With your Key Relationship. Re-roll to see where they live.

steps.action: Choose or roll: Human or Replicant

steps.details: D12 on archetype table. Determines key attribute, key skills, and chinyen die.

steps.action: Choose or roll Years on the Force
steps.details: D12. Replicants are always Rookies. Determines attribute/skill increase budgets.
Start all four at C. Spend increases (from Years). Key attribute must reach B or higher. Replicants get +1 increase for STR or AGL. Can drop one from C to D for an extra increase.
Health = ceil((STR_dieSize + AGL_dieSize)/4). Resolve = ceil((INT_dieSize + EMP_dieSize)/4). Replicants: Health +2, Resolve -2.
All start at D. Spend skill increases (from Years). Key skills from archetype should be prioritized. Max level A.
Number from Years on Force table. Pick from archetype suggestions or full list.
Roll on 5 memory tables (when, where, who, what, feeling) or design freely.
Roll on 3 relationship tables (who, quality, situation) or design freely. Give them a name.
Describe in a sentence or two. Use archetype suggestions or create your own.

steps.action: Roll starting Promotion and Chinyen Points
steps.details: Promotion: roll die from Years table. Chinyen: roll die from Archetype. Apply Replicant modifiers (-1 each).

attributeRules.extraIncrease: Can drop one attribute from C to D to gain one additional increase.
attributeRules.replicantBonus: +1 increase, must go to Strength or Agility.

skillRules.increaseStep: One level per increase (D to C, C to B, B to A).

quickPlayReference._comment: Minimal rules reference for a player handout or in-app cheat sheet.
howToRoll: Grab 2 dice: your Attribute die + your Skill die (each rated D6 to D12).
Roll both. Any result of 6 or higher is a SUCCESS.
A 10+ on a single die counts as TWO successes.
2+ total successes = Critical Success (bonus effect or extra damage).
Zero successes = Failure. You can PUSH the roll.
pushing: Re-roll any dice NOT showing a 1.
Each 1 on the pushed roll hurts you: damage (physical skills) or stress (mental skills).
Humans push once. Replicants push twice (but always take stress, even on physical rolls).
combat: Draw initiative cards (1-10). Act lowest to highest.
Close combat: opposed roll. Winner hits loser.
Ranged combat: straight FIREARMS roll. Cover gives shooter disadvantage.
2+ more successes than opponent = Critical Hit. Roll Crit Die.
damageAndStress: Damage reduces Health. Stress reduces Resolve.
Hit zero in either = Broken (out of action, can only crawl/mumble).
Signature item: recover 1 stress once per session.
investigation: 4 Shifts per day: Morning, Day, Evening, Night.
Each Shift you can visit ONE location.
Splitting the party is ENCOURAGED. More ground = more clues.
The Countdown ticks regardless. Don't waste Shifts.
Every 3 Shifts of work, you need 1 Shift of Downtime or suffer stress.
promotionVsHumanity: Promotion Points: earned by being a good cop (solving cases, following orders).
Humanity Points: earned by being a good person (compassion, key memory, relationships).
These two often conflict. That tension IS the game.

showConfig._comment: Per-show overrides for TMP live shows using the Blade Runner RPG system. This section would be customized for each Betawave Tape that uses Blade Runner.
